title: People using safely managed sanitation services (% of population)
definition: The percentage of people using improved sanitation facilities that are not shared with other households and where excreta are safely disposed of in situ or transported and treated offsite. Improved sanitation facilities include flush/pour flush to piped sewer systems, septic tanks or pit latrines: ventilated improved pit latrines, compositing toilets or pit latrines with slabs.

editorial.shortHook: A candidate map for safely managed sanitation.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Kuwait, Qatar, Switzerland, while the lightest include Ethiopia, Morocco, Niger; that contrast is the fastest way to read the safely managed sanitation pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Safely managed sanitation may add useful WORLDPRINT variety once source coverage, unit clarity, map interest, and distractor ambiguity are reviewed.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Rural basic sanitation can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Rural clean cooking access can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Safely managed drinking water can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Health maps often separate income, public-health capacity, and conflict exposure.
Check both high-income and low-income anchors.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Safely managed sanitation adds useful practice depth with an understandable unit.
Practice-only until distractor ambiguity and new-player comprehension are checked in playtests.
